structure.title: Federal Register NPRM Header + Agency/Action/Summary — HIPAA Security Rule Cybersecurity Strengtheni
section_path: Federal Register NPRM Header + Agency/Action/Summary — HIPAA Security Rule Cybersecurity Strengthening (45 CFR Parts 160 and 164)

metadata.summary: Identifies the issuing agency (HHS OCR), the regulatory vehicle (Notice of proposed rulemaking; notice of Tribal consultation), and the NPRM’s purpose: proposed modifications to the HIPAA Security Rule to better protect confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI. Describes drivers for change (breaches/cyberattacks, common compliance deficiencies, evolving care environment, cybersecurity best practices, and relevant court decisions) and frames affected parties as covered entities and business associates (“regulated entities”).

structure.title: Administrative Record and Public Posting Rules — Comment Inspection, Redaction/Rejection Criteria, D
section_path: Administrative Record and Public Posting Rules — Comment Inspection, Redaction/Rejection Criteria, Docket Access, and Tribal Registration Link

metadata.summary: Explains how comments will be made public on Regulations.gov and warns that postings may include personal information; outlines HHS’s ability to redact or reject specific non-substantive or prohibited content (e.g., threats, profanity, sensitive health information, copyrighted materials, third-party identifiers). Provides docket access instructions and the advance registration requirement/link for Tribal consultation participation.

structure.title: Table of Contents (Part 1) — Executive Summary + Statutory Authority and Regulatory History (HIPAA,
section_path: Table of Contents (Part 1) — Executive Summary + Statutory Authority and Regulatory History (HIPAA, HITECH, prior Security Rule rulemakings)

metadata.summary: Outlines the NPRM’s front-matter structure: Executive Summary (overview, applicability, abbreviations) and Statutory Authority/Regulatory History, including HIPAA and HITECH authority and the Security Rule’s regulatory milestones (1998 NPRM, 2003 final rule, 2009 delegation, 2013 omnibus rulemaking). Establishes where legal authority and historical amendments are discussed for later citation linking.

structure.title: Statutory/Regulatory Footnotes + Definitions Context — HIPAA/HITECH Citations and PHI/ePHI Scope Exclusions (45 CFR 160.103, 164 subparts)
section_path: Statutory/Regulatory Footnotes + Definitions Context — HIPAA/HITECH Citations and PHI/ePHI Scope Exclusions (45 CFR 160.103, 164 subparts)

metadata.summary: Provides legal and definitional grounding via footnoted citations: HIPAA Subtitle F authority (SSA sections 1171–1179), references to Privacy, Breach Notification, and Enforcement Rules, and key definitional distinctions (IIHI vs. PHI) including PHI exclusions (employment records, FERPA-related education/treatment records, decedent information >50 years). Also continues justification subpoints about evolving safeguards and maintaining flexibility/scalability.

structure.title: Section-by-Section Proposed Amendments Outline — Definitions and Security Standards (160.103, 164.304, 164.306, 164.308) (part 1/123)
section_path: Section-by-Section Proposed Amendments Outline — Definitions and Security Standards (160.103, 164.304, 164.306, 164.308)

metadata.summary: Begins the detailed outline for proposed Security Rule amendments: Section 160.103 definitions (current provision, issues, proposals, request for comment) and extensive Section 164.304 definition changes/additions (e.g., access, safeguards, authentication, availability/confidentiality, multi-factor authentication, risk/threat/vulnerability, technology asset, technical controls). Also introduces proposed revisions to general security standards (164.306) and administrative safeguards (164.308) with the standard sub-structure of current provisions, issues, proposals, and requests for comment.

metadata.summary: Begins the detailed outline for proposed Security Rule amendments: Section 160.103 definitions (current provision, issues, proposals, request for comment) and extensive Section 164.304 definition changes/additions (e.g., access, safeguards, authentication, availability/confidentiality, multi-factor authentication, risk/threat/vulnerability, technology asset, technical controls). Also introduces proposed revisions to general security standards (164.306) and administrative safeguards (164.308) with the standard sub-structure of current provisions, issues, proposals, and requests for comment.
